allFeatures.name: Fighting Style
allFeatures.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty.

allFeatures.name: Second Wind
allFeatures.description: You have a limited well of stamina that you can draw on to protect yourself from harm.

allFeatures.name: Action Surge
allFeatures.description: You can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment.

allFeatures.name: Martial Archetype
allFeatures.description: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that you strive to emulate in your combat styles and techniques.

subclasses.name: Champion
subclasses.description: The archetypal Champion focuses on the development of raw physical power honed to deadly perfection.

features.name: Improved Critical
features.description: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

features.name: Remarkable Athlete
features.description: You can add half your proficiency bonus (round up) to any Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution check you make that doesn't already use your proficiency bonus. In addition, when you make a running long jump, the distance you can cover increases by a number of feet equal to your Strength modifier.

features.name: Additional Fighting Style
features.description: You can choose a second option from the Fighting Style class feature.

features.name: Superior Critical
features.description: Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 18-20.

features.name: Survivor
features.description: At the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points equal to 5 + your Constitution modifier if you have no more than half of your hit points left. You don't gain this benefit if you have 0 hit points.

subclasses.name: Battle Master
subclasses.description: Those who emulate the Battle Master employ martial techniques passed down through generations. A Battle Master uses superiority dice to fuel powerful combat maneuvers.

features.name: Combat Superiority
features.description: You learn three maneuvers of your choice from: Trip Attack, Riposte, Precision Attack, Menacing Attack, Disarming Attack, Pushing Attack, Rally, Commander's Strike. You have four superiority dice, which are d8s. A superiority die is expended when you use it. You regain all expended superiority dice on a short or long rest.

features.name: Student of War
features.description: You gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice.

features.name: Know Your Enemy
features.description: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn whether the creature is your equal, superior, or inferior in regard to two of the following characteristics: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Armor Class, current hit points, total class levels, or fighter class levels.

features.name: Improved Combat Superiority
features.description: Your superiority dice turn into d10s.

features.name: Relentless
features.description: When you roll initiative and have no superiority dice remaining, you regain 1 superiority die.

features.name: Improved Combat Superiority
features.description: Your superiority dice turn into d12s.

subclasses.name: Eldritch Knight
subclasses.description: The Eldritch Knight combines martial prowess with careful study of magic, learning to cast wizard spells focused on abjuration and evocation.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You learn two wizard cantrips of your choice and gain the ability to cast wizard spells, drawing primarily from the abjuration and evocation schools.

features.name: Weapon Bond
features.description: You learn a ritual that creates a magical bond between yourself and one weapon. You can bond with up to two weapons. You can't be disarmed of a bonded weapon unless you are incapacitated, and you can summon a bonded weapon to your hand as a bonus action.

features.name: War Magic
features.description: When you use your action to cast a cantrip, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.

features.name: Eldritch Strike
features.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, that creature has disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

features.name: Arcane Charge
features.description: When you use Action Surge, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can teleport before or after the additional action.

features.name: Improved War Magic
features.description: When you use your action to cast a spell, you can make one weapon attack as a bonus action.
